originalTitle: Cyberdecks used to look like little laptops, but now they’re getting more personal
summary: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The Cut and Wired, went viral earlier this year with a mermaid-themed cyberdeck she made inside an old purse. She has since […]
content: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn’t look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The Cut and Wired, went viral earlier this year with a mermaid-themed cyberdeck she made inside an old purse. She has since upgraded it and built an MP3 player and a solar-powered cyberdeck.  Tan and countless other DIYers are attracting millions of views showing off the personal computers they’ve built inside purses, jewelry boxes, toys, and old tech, hiding Raspberry Pi boards inside art projects. [Media: https://www.tiktok.com/@ubeboobey/video/7615659061199080726] [Media: http://tiktok.com/@ubeboobey/video/7622993723038666006] [Media: https://www.tiktok.com/@cocoasaesthetic_/video/7625346057433550094?_r=1&_t=ZP-95cwxfSpKfP] The colorful, quirky builds popping up across social media are a drastic shift away from the typical look the cyberdecks we’ve featured have had, which often consisted of a 3D-printed chassis or a rugged box like a Pelican case, usually with a cyberpunk-style design.  Inside, these homemade devices are essentially mini Linux computers for specific tasks, usually done offline, like reading, journaling, or listening to music. But now, a cyberdeck doesn’t have to look like a computer at all. [Media: https://www.tiktok.com/@krisydays/video/7635516152898178312?q=cyberdeck&t=1780595579900] [Media: https://www.tiktok.com/@tomstantom4/video/7639397953853066527?q=cyberdeck&t=1780595579900] This new movement emphasizes using recycled or thrifted parts and leans more into the artistic side of making a cyberdeck. One TikTok user who goes by @diypagancrafts created a cyberdeck out of a retro TV-themed purse with the screen on the outside and the Raspberry Pi, keyboard, and a game controller stored inside. Almost anything with enough space for a single-board computer and a screen can be a cyberdeck — a Dunkin’ Donuts munchkin box, a Pokeball, an Altoids tin, a plastic box stuffed with fake moss and flowers.
chineseTitle: Cyber​​decks 曾经看起来像小型笔记本电脑，但现在它们变得更加个性化
chineseSummary: DIY 爱好者和 TikTok 用户 Annike Tan（昵称 @ubeboobey）可以随身携带她的 cyberdeck，而不会引起任何人注意，因为它看起来根本不像一台电脑。谭曾在《The Cut》和《连线》中出现过，今年早些时候，她在一个旧钱包里制作的美人鱼主题的网络甲板走红了。她从那时起[…]
chineseContent: DIY 爱好者和 TikTok 用户 Annike Tan（昵称为 @ubeboobey）可以随身携带她的 cyberdeck，而不会引起任何人注意，因为它看起来根本不像一台电脑。谭曾在《The Cut》和《连线》中出现过，今年早些时候，她在一个旧钱包里制作的美人鱼主题的网络甲板走红了。此后，她对其进行了升级，并建造了一个 MP3 播放器和一个太阳能供电的网络甲板。  Tan 和无数其他 DIY 爱好者展示了他们在钱包、珠宝盒、玩具和旧科技中内置的个人电脑，并将 Raspberry Pi 板隐藏在艺术项目中，吸引了数百万次观看。 [媒体：https://www.tiktok.com/@ubeboobey/video/7615659061199080726] [媒体：http://tiktok.com/@ubeboobey/video/7622993723038666006] [媒体： https://www.tiktok.com/@cocoasaesthetic_/video/7625346057433550094?_r=1&_t=ZP-95cwxfSpKfP]社交媒体上出现的色彩缤纷、古怪的建筑与我们所展示的 Cyberdeck 的典型外观截然不同，后者通常由 3D 打印的底盘或坚固的盒子组成就像鹈鹕箱一样，通常采用赛博朋克风格的设计。  在内部，这些自制设备本质上是用于特定任务的迷你 Linux 计算机，通常离线完成，例如阅读、写日记或听音乐。但现在，Cyber​​deck 根本不必看起来像计算机。 [媒体：https://www.tiktok.com/@krisydays/video/7635516152898178312?q=cyberdeck&t=1780595579900] [媒体： https://www.tiktok.com/@tomstantom4/video/7639397953853066527?q=cyberdeck&t=1780595579900] 这一新运动强调使用回收或节俭的部件，并更倾向于制作 Cyberdeck 的艺术方面。一位名为 @diypagancrafts 的 TikTok 用户用复古电视主题的钱包创建了一个 Cyber​​deck，钱包的外面是屏幕，钥匙是 Raspberry Pi
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
